title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
content: “The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.”
action: Record: Convert every external obstacle into a required component of your operational process.

title: Letters from a Stoic
author: Seneca
content: “We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.”
action: Record: Audit your anxieties; most are non-data-backed simulations of failure that waste cognitive capacity.

title: The War of Art
author: Steven Pressfield
content: “Most of us have two lives. The life we live, and the unlived life within us. The resistance between the two is the work.”
action: Record: Define the friction you feel as the primary indicator that you are engaging with high-value, high-impact work.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor Frankl
content: “When we are no longer able to change a situation, we are challenged to change ourselves.”
action: Record: When the environment is immutable, pivot from tactical adjustment to internal structural hardening.

title: The Book of Five Rings
author: Miyamoto Musashi
content: “Do nothing which is of no use.”
action: Record: Eliminate every action, thought, or asset that does not directly contribute to the stability or expansion of your primary objective.
